overview: Esper Vial is a Legacy tempo deck that leverages Aether Vial to deploy threats untapped and maintain board pressure. Quantum Riddler and Yorion, Sky Nomad provide explosive early-game value, evasion, and untap synergy to outpace opponents. The deck aims to control the middle game with interaction while closing out matches with relentless flying attackers.
win_conditions: Overwhelm the opponent with untapped flying creatures played via Aether Vial
Generate infinite value and attacks by repeatedly untapping Quantum Riddler and Aether Vial
Pressure the opponent with Yorion, Sky Nomad's evasion and card exile ability

keep_criteria: Hands containing Aether Vial and at least two early spells or creatures
Hands with Quantum Riddler and sufficient lands to cast it by turn two
Hands featuring Yorion, Sky Nomad alongside interaction or acceleration
Keep hands with 2-3 lands, Aether Vial, and at least one 1-2 CMC creature or removal spell.
Keep hands that can play a land and a 1-drop or Vial on turn 1.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking Aether Vial or any form of acceleration
Hands with only late-game cards or high-mana creatures
Hands with insufficient lands to cast turn-one or turn-two threats
Mulligan hands with Vial but no creatures/removal, 4+ lands with no early plays, or hands lacking interaction against known tempo/combo threats.

early_game: Play Aether Vial on turn one to enable untapped threats
Deploy Quantum Riddler or Yorion, Sky Nomad to establish immediate board presence
Use basic interaction to protect your early advantages
Turn 1: Play land, cast Aether Vial or a 1-drop creature.
Turn 2: Play land, cast a second land or cheap creature/removal.
If you have Vial + a cheap creature, Vial the creature on turn 2 or 3 to establish board presence while preserving mana for interaction.
Prioritize developing a defensive wall of threats while holding up spot removal or counters for key metagame threats like Delver or Tron engines.
mid_game: Leverage Quantum Riddler's entry ability to untap Aether Vial and lands for additional plays
Maintain tempo by countering key opponent threats and playing Yorion with flying exiles
Cycle through your hand to find removal and card draw while keeping pressure
Use Aether Vial to cast creatures from hand when you have excess mana, effectively converting mana into tempo.
Avoid overextending into known sweepers or heavy removal.
Balance hand size by using Yorion triggers to refill, and manage your graveyard carefully if the format features graveyard hate.
Prioritize keeping interaction for the opponent's win conditions rather than burning it on low-impact threats.
Commit threats when you have interaction to protect them or when the opponent is tapped out.
Hold back if they have sweepers, counters, or heavy removal in hand.
late_game: Stabilize the board by repeatedly untapping Quantum Riddler and Aether Vial
Use Yorion, Sky Nomad to exile opponent's blockers or give your creatures flying
Close the game with a flurry of untapped attackers while holding up counterspells
Yorion, Sky Nomad is the primary closer.
Cast it to draw a card and potentially cheat a game-winning permanent onto the battlefield.
Secondary finishers include mid-4 CMC creatures Vial'd into play or late-game value engines that out-resource the opponent.
Go all-in when you have board control, sufficient interaction to protect Yorion, and the opponent is low on resources or has no sweepers.
Commit when you can Vial multiple threats to close the game or when the opponent is tapped out for a key spell.
Also commit when you can force a favorable trade that leaves you ahead in board state.

key_cards.name: Aether Vial
key_cards.role: Core enabler for untapped creature deployment and untap synergy
key_cards.notes: Play it early to drop threats without tapping lands; use its untap ability to fuel Quantum Riddler loops

key_cards.name: Yorion, Sky Nomad
key_cards.role: Flying threat and card advantage engine
key_cards.notes: Exile cards from hand to give flying to other creatures or protect Yorion; play untapped via Vial for immediate pressure

key_cards.name: Quantum Riddler
key_cards.notes: Enter with a counter to untap two permanents; pair with Aether Vial to play multiple creatures in a single turn

sequencing_tips: Always play Aether Vial before your creatures to maximize untapped entries
Use Quantum Riddler's triggered ability to untap Aether Vial and a land, then play another creature
Hold up counterspells for opponent's board wipes or key threats before committing your Yorion
Exile cards with Yorion only when you need flying evasion or to protect a key permanent
Common mistakes include overusing Vial on low-impact creatures, forgetting to hold up interaction, and mismanaging Yorion timing.
Remember that creatures Vial'd into play enter tapped, so plan your turns accordingly.
Synergize Yorion with cheap threats to maximize the 'put a permanent from hand' trigger.
Always track the opponent's hand for sweepers and counters, and adjust your Vial timing to exploit their tapped-out turns.
In Unknown format, adapt quickly to meta shifts and prioritize flexibility over rigid game plans.

matchups.advice: Use Aether Vial to play Yorion and Quantum Riddler early to race their damage output; prioritize countering their finishers.
Apply steady pressure with untapped threats to force counterspells; use Quantum Riddler to untap and play around their hand size.
Disrupt their assembly with counterspells and removal; use Yorion's flying to pressure while they search for pieces.

Use Vial to cast creatures when opponents are tapped out or after they've used counters. Deploy cheap, efficient threats that are hard to counter (flying, hexproof, or multiple targets). Save interaction for their key spells and avoid overcommitting to value when they have counterspells in hand.

Apply early pressure to disrupt mana development. Use spot removal on key ramp creatures and Vial threats to force trades before they stabilize. Don't let them reach their late-game power level; pressure them into mistakes and close the game before they can deploy their engines.

Win the resource war by prioritizing card advantage and efficient Vial plays. Target their key threats with removal and control the board to force them to overextend. Track their hand for interaction and adjust your threat deployment accordingly. Win the late game by out-valuing them with Yorion and superior resource management.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers, counterspells, and combo pieces.
Note when opponents tap out for big threats or engines.
Pay attention to Tron/Drake mana bases and Sneak & Show hand disruption.
Adjust your Vial timing based on their available interaction and board state.
